summary: Verified fix for CMS HTML rendering (Vision, Mission, About the Association on About page + home about descriptions). After a frontend restart to pick up the code changes, the raw HTML tags are no longer visible as text — HTML content is rendered properly with bold/italic/color/heading styling via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. CMS Admin toolbar (Bold, Italic, Highlight Blue/Green/Red, Heading, List, Link) is visible for Vision, Mission, and About Content fields.

integration_issues.issue:
News item content (

{item.content}

) is still rendered via text interpolation. If admin enters HTML markup (e.g. ) in news content, it appears as raw text on the homepage. Separate from the current fix scope but worth noting.

action_items: OBSERVATION: The dev server did not hot-reload the CMS render fix until a manual `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend`. After restart, the fix works as expected. Main agent should be aware that in some cases a restart is required for fresh preview.
OPTIONAL (separate from this fix): Consider using dangerouslySetInnerHTML (or a sanitizer) for news item content on HomePage line 355 if news is intended to accept rich text.
critical_code_review_comments: Using dangerouslySetInnerHTML directly with CMS content trusts admin input entirely. Since CMS is admin-authenticated (super_admin), risk is limited, but ideally sanitize HTML (e.g. DOMPurify) before rendering to defend against a compromised admin account.

The RichTextArea component allows the admin to inject arbitrary HTML including scripts if desired (though the toolbar only inserts safe tags). Add a server-side or client-side sanitizer for defense in depth.
test_credentials: admin@idsea.org / Admin@123 (used for CMS admin verification)
seed_data_creation: Temporarily set cms.vision, cms.mission, cms.about_content to HTML strings and home page_content about_description/2 to HTML strings for verification, then RESTORED to original empty/plain-text values after testing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The fix for HTML rendering in Vision/Mission/About/Home works after frontend restart. Public CMS endpoints return HTML strings correctly, and public pages render them as HTML (with <strong>, <em>, <span style=color>, <h3> tags in the DOM). Toolbar in CMSAdmin renders correctly for HTML formatting.
rca of the issue:
Root cause was E1's earlier commit changing {text} to dangerouslySetInnerHTML in AboutPage.jsx (lines 71, 82, 94) and HomePage.jsx (lines 209-213). Verified the code change is present and functional in browser. The dev server did not hot-reload the change initially — one initial test showed the OLD behavior (escaped HTML text in

tag). After sudo supervisorctl restart frontend, the correct behavior (DIV with //

child nodes) was observed. Recommendation: the code fix is correct; the transient dev-server state issue does not require code changes.
